title: Abort: `assert(!queue->alive)` in `_queue_clear` (`_interpqueuesmodule.c:559`)
description: `Modules/_interpqueuesmodule.c`: in `queue_create`, when `_queues_add()` fails to allocate its `_queueref` under OOM, the error path calls `_queue_clear()` on the still-alive queue (it skips `_queue_kill_and_wait()`), so `_queue_clear`'s `assert(!queue->alive)` fires and aborts
notes: queue_create (L1089) calls _queue_init (sets queue->alive=1, L547) then _queues_add (L1102). Under OOM _queues_add's GLOBAL_MALLOC(_queueref) (L916) fails, returns ERR_QUEUE_ALLOC (<0); queue_create then calls _queue_clear(queue) (L1104) whose first stmt assert(!queue->alive) (L559) fires because the queue is still alive. All correct teardowns reach _queue_clear via _queue_free only after _queue_kill_and_wait sets alive=0 (cf. queue_destroy L1119); the create error path skips that. Not a UAF: _queue_clear's actual work (free items/lock, zero struct) is correct for a fresh never-registered queue, so on NDEBUG the path raises a clean MemoryError. assert-based abort -> fires on assertion builds (ft_debug_asan AND jit); ft_release/upstream compile the assert out and exit cleanly (n/a). Fix: set queue->alive=0 before _queue_clear in queue_create's _queues_add-failure path (or route via _queue_kill_and_wait/_queue_free). Minimal repro start=2: alloc #0=_queue struct, #1=mutex (succeed -> alive), #2=_queueref in _queues_add (fails). unboundop=3 is UNBOUND. 3 vehicles all call concurrent.interpreters._queues.create under OOM.
